pool_brief: You are given a starter HTML file for a Nigerian business scenario. Write script.js to process and log the string data using template literals, string search methods, and string transformation methods. Your script.js must: (1) use template literals (backticks with ${}) for all output statements — do not concatenate strings with +; (2) build at least one multi-line template literal string that formats a confirmation or summary message; (3) use at least two search methods (includes(), startsWith(), endsWith(), or indexOf()) on the raw string data and log the result of each check with a descriptive label; (4) use at least two transformation methods (trim(), toLowerCase(), toUpperCase(), replace(), replaceAll(), or slice()) to clean the raw inputs, and log the cleaned value alongside the raw value to show the difference; (5) use const for all raw inputs and all cleaned results. Do not modify the HTML file.

project_pool.context: A food delivery order confirmation page for a Chowdeck customer in Lagos with messy registration input that needs cleaning before display.

files.content:
Chowdeck

Fast food. Faster delivery.

Customer Details (raw input)

Amara Obi

AMARA.OBI@GMAIL.COM

081-234-567-890

14 Bode Thomas Street, Surulere, Lagos

Order Summary

Restaurant: Mr. Bigg's Lekki

Order reference: CDK-2025-004482

1x Beef Burger Combo, 2x Pepsi 35cl

Total: ₦4,800

criteria.description: All console.log calls use template literals with backticks and ${} — no string concatenation with +; at least one multi-line template literal builds a formatted order confirmation showing the cleaned name, email, phone, restaurant, order reference, and total; expressions inside ${} are used (not just variable names) for at least one output

At least two search methods applied to the string data and logged: includes('@') on the email returns true; endsWith('.com') on the cleaned email returns true; indexOf('@') is used (typically combined with slice) to locate the @ sign; results are logged with descriptive labels showing what each check confirms

At least two transformation methods applied to clean the raw inputs: rawEmail.trim().toLowerCase() produces 'amara.obi@gmail.com'; rawName.trim() produces 'Amara Obi'; rawPhone.replaceAll('-', '') produces '081234567890'; slice() with indexOf('@') extracts the email domain ('gmail.com'); the cleaned values are logged so the before/after difference is visible

camelCase naming throughout; const used for rawName, rawEmail, rawPhone, and all cleaned results; no var; no string concatenation with +; all console.log calls have clear descriptive labels; no bare magic values (the search strings like '@' and '.com' appear directly in method calls, which is fine)

project_pool.context: A seller onboarding page for a new Konga marketplace store. The seller submitted their details with inconsistent casing and a product listing URL that needs to be parsed.

files.content:
Konga

Nigeria's Trusted Online Marketplace

Seller Details (raw input)

TECH HAVEN STORE

TECHHAVEN@KONGA.NG

090-345-678-901

KNG-SEL-2025-04482

First Product Listing

https://konga.com/product/tecno-spark-20-pro-256gb

Price: ₦89,000

Category: Phones & Tablets

Stock: 12 units

criteria.description: All console.log calls use template literals; at least one multi-line template literal builds a formatted seller profile card showing the cleaned store name, email, phone, and seller code; at least one expression (not just a variable) appears inside ${} — e.g. a method call or an operation

At least two search methods applied and logged: startsWith('https://') on the product URL returns true; includes('konga.com') on the product URL returns true; endsWith('.ng') on the cleaned email returns true; indexOf('/product/') is used with slice() to extract the product slug from the URL; results logged with labels

At least two transformation methods applied: rawStoreName.trim().toLowerCase() produces 'tech haven store' (or trim only for display); rawEmail.trim().toLowerCase() produces 'techhaven@konga.ng'; rawPhone.replaceAll('-', '') produces '090345678901'; slice() used with indexOf('/product/') to extract the product slug ('tecno-spark-20-pro-256gb'); cleaned values logged alongside the raw inputs

camelCase naming; const for all raw inputs and cleaned results; no var; no string concatenation with +; all logs have descriptive labels (e.g. 'Clean email:', 'Product slug:', 'Is valid URL:'); consistent indentation

project_pool.context: A payment receipt page for a Paystack transaction. The customer name and email were submitted in inconsistent format, and the payment description uses dashes that need to be replaced for display.

files.content:
Paystack

Simple, powerful payments for Africa

Transaction Details

Reference: PSK_LIVE_20250422_0044821

Merchant: Publica Academy

Amount: ₦25,000

Description: course-enrollment-javascript-fundamentals

Status: SUCCESS

Customer Details (raw input)

BAYO ADEYEMI

BAYO.ADEYEMI@YAHOO.COM

0802-456-789-01

criteria.description: All console.log calls use template literals; at least one multi-line template literal builds a formatted receipt showing the cleaned customer name, email, transaction reference, merchant, amount, and cleaned description; no + concatenation used anywhere

At least two search methods applied and logged with labels: startsWith('PSK_') on the transaction reference returns true; includes('LIVE') on the reference confirms it is a live transaction (not test); endsWith('.com') on the cleaned email returns true; results clearly logged so the validation purpose is visible

At least two transformation methods applied: rawName.trim().toLowerCase() or rawName.trim() produces the clean name; rawEmail.trim().toLowerCase() produces 'bayo.adeyemi@yahoo.com'; rawPhone.replaceAll('-', '') produces '080245678901'; description.replaceAll('-', ' ') produces a readable label ('course enrollment javascript fundamentals'); cleaned values logged showing before/after

camelCase naming; const for all raw inputs and all cleaned results; no var; no string concatenation with +; all console.log calls have descriptive labels; the transaction reference and description are stored in named const variables before being passed to methods

project_pool.context: A student registration confirmation page for Publica Academy. The student's name and email were submitted with spaces and wrong casing, and a student ID has been assigned that needs to be parsed.

files.content:
Publica Academy

Learn. Build. Grow.

Student Details (raw input)

Ngozi Eze

NGOZI.EZE@PUBLICAACADEMY.COM

0803-456-789-01

Student ID: PUB-JS2-2025-0044

Enrolled Course

JavaScript Fundamentals

Modules: 18

Start date: 2025-05-01

Course fee: ₦35,000

criteria.description: All console.log calls use template literals; at least one multi-line template literal builds a formatted enrollment confirmation showing the student's clean name, email, student ID, course name, and start date — styled like a real confirmation screen; no string concatenation with + anywhere

At least two search methods applied and logged: includes('@') on the email returns true; endsWith('.com') on the cleaned email returns true; startsWith('PUB-') on the student ID confirms it is a valid Publica ID (true); indexOf('@') used with slice() to extract the email domain; results logged with descriptive labels

At least two transformation methods applied: rawEmail.trim().toLowerCase() produces 'ngozi.eze@publicaacademy.com'; rawName.trim() produces 'Ngozi Eze'; rawPhone.replaceAll('-', '') produces '080345678901'; slice(0, cleanName.indexOf(' ')) extracts the first name ('Ngozi'); slice(cleanEmail.indexOf('@') + 1) extracts the domain; cleaned values logged with before/after comparison visible

camelCase naming; const for all raw inputs and computed values; no var; no + concatenation; all logs have labels; the student ID string ('PUB-JS2-2025-0044') is stored in a named const variable before methods are called on it; consistent indentation throughout

project_pool.context: A driver onboarding confirmation page for Bolt in Lagos. The driver's name and email were entered with inconsistent casing, the phone has dashes, and the vehicle plate number has extra spaces.

files.content:
Bolt

Your city. Your ride.

Driver Details (raw input)

EMEKA OKAFOR

EMEKA.OKAFOR@GMAIL.COM

081-234-567-890

LSD 234 AA

Vehicle & Trip Info

Vehicle: Toyota Corolla 2019

City: Lagos

Driver reference: BLT-LGS-2025-0044

Trips completed: 312

Average rating: 4.88

criteria.description: All console.log calls use template literals; at least one multi-line template literal builds a formatted driver profile card showing clean name, email, phone, plate number, driver reference, city, and vehicle; no string concatenation with + anywhere; at least one expression inside ${} performs an operation (e.g. a method call or a slice)

At least two search methods applied and logged: startsWith('BLT-') on the driver reference confirms it is a valid Bolt reference (true); includes('LGS') on the driver reference confirms the driver is registered in Lagos (true); endsWith('.com') on the cleaned email returns true; indexOf('@') used with slice() to extract the email domain ('gmail.com'); results logged with labels

At least two transformation methods applied: rawName.trim().toLowerCase() or rawName.trim() produces 'EMEKA OKAFOR' trimmed / 'emeka okafor' lowercased; rawEmail.trim().toLowerCase() produces 'emeka.okafor@gmail.com'; rawPhone.replaceAll('-', '') produces '081234567890'; rawPlate.trim() produces 'LSD 234 AA'; slice(0, 3) on the clean phone extracts the network prefix ('081'); all cleaned values logged alongside the raw versions

camelCase naming; const for all raw inputs and cleaned results; no var; no string concatenation with +; all console.log calls have descriptive labels; the driver reference ('BLT-LGS-2025-0044') stored in a named const before methods are called on it; consistent indentation
